volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 549 - Section 653a)

sections.label: Section 549
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἄναξ ὅδʼ ἡμὶν Αἰγέως γόνος
sections.translation: Look , there comes our lord , Theseus son of Aegeus ,

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: Θησεὺς κατʼ ὀμφὴν σὴν ἐφʼ ἁστάλη πάρα .
sections.translation: at the sound of your voice , to do that for which he was summoned .

sections.label: Section 551
sections.original: πολλῶν ἀκούων ἔν τε τῷ πάρος χρόνῳ
sections.translation: Through hearing from many in the past about the bloody marring of your sight , I recognized it was you , son of Laius ; and now on coming here , through sight I am more fully certain .

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: σκευή τε γάρ σε καὶ τὸ δύστηνον κάρα
sections.translation: For your clothing and that heart-rending face alike assure me that it is you . And in all compassion I ask you , ill-fated Oedipus , with what petition to the city and to me have you taken your place here , you and the poor maiden at your side . Declare it . Dire indeed must be the fortune which you tell ,

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: δίδασκε · δεινὴν γάρ τινʼ ἂν πρᾶξιν τύχοις
sections.translation: for me to stand aloof from it ; since I know that I myself also was reared in exile , just as you , and that in foreign lands I wrestled with perils to my life , like no other man .

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: ὥστε ξένον γʼ ἂν οὐδένʼ ὄνθʼ , ὥσπερ σὺ νῦν ,
sections.translation: Never , then , would I turn aside from a stranger , such as you are now , or refuse to help in his deliverance . For I know well that I am a man , and that my portion of tomorrow is no greater than yours .

sections.label: Section 569
sections.original: Θησεῦ , τὸ σὸν γενναῖον ἐν σμικρῷ λόγῳ
sections.translation: Theseus , in a few words your nobleness has come to such a point

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: παρῆκεν , ὥστε βραχέα μοι δεῖσθαι φράσαι .
sections.translation: that I need make only a brief reply . You have said who I am , from what father I am sprung , and from what land I have come ; and so nothing else remains for me but to speak my wish , and the tale is told .

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: τοῦτʼ αὐτὸ νῦν δίδασχʼ , ὅπως ἂν ἐκμάθω .
sections.translation: Then inform me of this very thing , so that I may learn it .

sections.label: Section 576
sections.original: δώσων ἱκάνω τοὐμὸν ἄθλιον δέμας
sections.translation: I come to offer you my care-worn body as a gift — not one fine to look on , but the gains from it are better than beauty .

sections.label: Section 579
sections.original: ποῖον δὲ κέρδος ἀξιοῖς ἥκειν φέρων ;
sections.translation: And what gain do you claim to have brought ?

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: χρόνῳ μάθοις ἄν , οὐχὶ τῷ παρόντι που .
sections.translation: Later you may learn it — but not yet .

sections.label: Section 581
sections.original: ποίῳ γὰρ ἡ σὴ προσφορὰ δηλώσεται ;
sections.translation: At what time , then , will the benefit become clear ?

sections.label: Section 582
sections.original: ὅταν θάνω ʼγὼ καὶ σύ μου ταφεὺς γένῃ
sections.translation: When I am dead , and you have given me burial .

sections.label: Section 583
sections.original: τὰ λοίσθιʼ αἰτεῖ τοῦ βίου , τὰ δʼ ἐν μέσῳ
sections.translation: You crave life ’ s last service ; but for all between you have no memory , or no care .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: ἐνταῦθα γάρ μοι κεῖνα συγκομίζεται .
sections.translation: Indeed , for by that service I gather in all the rest .

sections.label: Section 586
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐν βραχεῖ δὴ τήνδε μʼ ἐξαιτεῖ χάριν .
sections.translation: Well then , this favor you crave from me is brief indeed .

sections.label: Section 587
sections.original: ὅρα γε μήν · οὐ σμικρός , οὔχ , ἁγὼν ὅδε .
sections.translation: Yet take care ; the struggle here is no light one . No , indeed .

sections.label: Section 588
sections.original: πότερα τὰ τῶν σῶν ἐκγόνων κἀμοῦ λέγεις ;—
sections.translation: Do you mean in respect to your sons , or to me ?

sections.label: Section 589
sections.original: κεῖνοι κομίζειν κεῖσʼ ἄναξ , χρῄζουσί με .
sections.translation: They will compel you to convey me there to Thebes .

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ θέλοντά γʼ οὐδὲ σοὶ φεύγειν καλόν .
sections.translation: But if you are willing , then exile is not becoming .

sections.label: Section 591
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδʼ , ὅτʼ αὐτὸς ἤθελον , παρίεσαν .
sections.translation: No , when I was willing , they refused .

sections.label: Section 592
sections.original: ὦ μῶρε , θυμὸς δʼ ἐν κακοῖς οὐ ξύμφορον .
sections.translation: Foolish man , anger amidst woes is not suitable .

sections.label: Section 593
sections.original: ὅταν μάθῃς μου , νουθέτει , τανῦν δʼ ἔα .
sections.translation: When you have heard my story , admonish ; till then , forbear .

sections.label: Section 594
sections.original: δίδασκʼ · ἄνευ γνώμης γὰρ οὔ με χρὴ λέγειν .
sections.translation: Speak . I must not pronounce without knowledge .

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: πέπονθα , Θησεῦ , δεινὰ πρὸς κακοῖς κακά .
sections.translation: I have suffered , Theseus , terrible woes upon woes .

sections.label: Section 596
sections.original: ἦ τὴν παλαιὰν ξυμφορὰν γένους ἐρεῖς ;
sections.translation: Will you speak of the ancient trouble of your race ?

sections.label: Section 597
sections.original: οὐ δῆτʼ , ἐπεὶ πᾶς τοῦτό γʼ Ἑλλήνων θροεῖ .
sections.translation: No , indeed ; all Greeks speak of that .

sections.label: Section 598
sections.original: τί γὰρ τὸ μεῖζον ἢ κατʼ ἄνθρωπον νοσεῖς ;
sections.translation: How , then , do you suffer beyond what is mortal ?

sections.label: Section 599
sections.original: οὕτως ἔχει μοι . γῆς ἐμῆς ἀπηλάθην
sections.translation: The circumstance is this : from my country I have been driven by my own sons ;

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: πρὸς τῶν ἐμαυτοῦ σπερμάτων · ἔστιν δέ μοι
sections.translation: and I may not return , since I am guilty of a father ’ s blood .

sections.label: Section 602
sections.original: πῶς δῆτα σʼ ἂν πεμψαίαθʼ , ὥστʼ οἰκεῖν δίχα ;
sections.translation: Why would they have you brought back , if you must dwell apart ?

sections.label: Section 603
sections.original: τὸ θεῖον αὐτοὺς ἐξαναγκάσει στόμα .
sections.translation: The word of the god will compel them .

sections.label: Section 604
sections.original: ποῖον πάθος δείσαντας ἐκ χρηστηρίων ;
sections.translation: What suffering do they fear from the oracles ?

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: ὅτι σφʼ ἀνάγκη τῇδε πληγῆναι χθονί .
sections.translation: That they must be struck down in this land .

sections.label: Section 606
sections.original: καὶ πῶς γένοιτʼ ἂν τἀμὰ κἀκείνων πικρά ;
sections.translation: And how should bitterness come between them and me ?

sections.label: Section 607
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ Αἰγέως παῖ , μόνοις οὐ γίγνεται
sections.translation: Dearest son of Aegeus , to the gods alone old age and death never come , but everything else sinks into chaos from time which overpowers all .

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: φθίνει μὲν ἰσχὺς γῆς , φθίνει δὲ σώματος ,
sections.translation: Earth ’ s strength decays , and so too the strength of the body ; trust dies ; distrust is born ; and the same spirit is never steadfast among friends , or between city and city . For some now , for others tomorrow , sweet feelings turn to bitter , and then once more to being dear .

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: τὰ τερπνὰ πικρὰ γίγνεται καὖθις φίλα .
sections.translation: And if now the sun shines brightly between Thebes and you , yet time in his course gives birth to days and nights untold , in which from a small cause they will

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: δόρει διασκεδῶσιν ἐκ σμικροῦ λόγου ·
sections.translation:
scatter with the spear today ’ s pledges of concord . Then one day my slumbering and buried corpse , cold in death , will drink their warm blood , if Zeus is still Zeus , and Phoebus , the son of Zeus , speaks clear .
But , since I would not break silence concerning words that must not spoken , allow me to cease where I began .

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: ἔα μʼ ἐν οἷσιν ἠρξάμην , τὸ σὸν μόνον
sections.translation: Only keep your own pledge good , and never will you say that in vain you welcomed Oedipus to dwell in this land — if indeed the gods do not deceive me .

sections.label: Section 629
sections.original: ἄναξ , πάλαι καὶ ταῦτα καὶ τοιαῦτʼ ἔπη
sections.translation: Lord , from the first this man has shown a

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: γῇ τῇδʼ ὅδʼ ἁνὴρ ὡς τελῶν ἐφαίνετο .
sections.translation: will to bring these words , or similar ones , to completion for our land .

sections.label: Section 631
sections.original: τίς δῆτʼ ἂν ἀνδρὸς εὐμένειαν ἐκβάλοι
sections.translation: Who , then , would reject the goodwill of such a one ? To whom , first , the hearth of a spear-friend is always available on our side , by reciprocal right ; then too he has come as a suppliant to our gods ,

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: γῇ τῇδε κἀμοὶ δασμὸν οὐ σμικρὸν τίνει .
sections.translation: paying no small recompense to this land and to me . In reverence for these claims , I will never spurn his favor , and I will establish a dwelling for him as a citizen in the land . And if it is the pleasure of the stranger to remain here , I will command you to

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: τόδʼ ἡδύ , τούτων , Οἰδίπους , δίδωμί σοι
sections.translation: protect him ; or , if it pleases him , to come with me . This choice or that , Oedipus , you may take ; your desire will be mine .

sections.label: Section 642
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , διδοίης τοῖσι τοιούτοισιν εὖ .
sections.translation: O Zeus , may you be good to men such as these !

sections.label: Section 643
sections.original: τί δῆτα χρῄζεις ; ἦ δόμους στείχειν ἐμούς ;
sections.translation: What is your wish , then ? Will you come to my house ?

sections.label: Section 644
sections.original: εἴ μοι θέμις γʼ ἦν · ἀλλʼ ὁ χῶρός ἐσθʼ ὅδε ,
sections.translation: Yes , I would , if it were right . But this is the place —

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: ἐν ᾧ τί πράξεις ; οὐ γὰρ ἀντιστήσομαι .
sections.translation: What will you do here ? Speak , for I will not hinder you .

sections.label: Section 646
sections.original: ἐν ᾧ κρατήσω τῶν ἔμʼ ἐκβεβληκότων .
sections.translation: — Where I will conquer those who cast me out .

sections.label: Section 647
sections.original: μέγʼ ἂν λέγοις δώρημα τῆς συνουσίας .
sections.translation: The promised gift of your presence would be great .

sections.label: Section 648
sections.original: εἰ σοί γʼ ἅπερ φῂς ἐμμενεῖ τελοῦντί μοι .
sections.translation: It shall be , if you keep your pledge with me .

sections.label: Section 649
sections.original: θάρσει τὸ τοῦδέ γʼ ἀνδρός · οὔ σε μὴ προδῶ .
sections.translation: Have courage concerning me ; never will I betray you .

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: οὔτοι σʼ ὑφʼ ὅρκου γʼ ὡς κακὸν πιστώσομαι .
sections.translation: I will not bind you with an oath as if an evil man .

sections.label: Section 651
sections.original: οὔκουν πέρα γʼ ἂν οὐδὲν ἢ λόγῳ φέροις .
sections.translation: Well , you would win nothing more than by my word .

sections.label: Section 652
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ποήσεις ;
sections.translation: What will you do , then ?

sections.label: Section 652a
sections.original: τοῦ μάλιστʼ ὄκνος σʼ ἔχει ;
sections.translation: What is it that you fear ?

sections.label: Section 653
sections.original: ἥξουσιν ἄνδρες
sections.translation: Men will come —

sections.label: Section 653a
sections.original: ἀλλὰ τοῖσδʼ ἔσται μέλον .
sections.translation: But these men here will see to that .
